quiz.title: Unit 2: Command of Evidence — Quantitative Basics (Quiz 5)
quiz.subtitle: Reading and Interpreting Graphs (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question:
A research team monitored the population of a protected bird species following a 2015 conservation initiative. The graph tracks population changes from 2012 to 2022.

Which choice best describes the trend shown in the graph?
options: The bird population declined steadily for the entire ten-year observation period.
The population reached its lowest point in 2014 before increasing annually through 2022.
Growth in the bird population remained constant between 2012 and 2016.
The conservation initiative caused a temporary spike in 2016 that returned to baseline levels by 2022.
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly identifies the V-shaped recovery pattern: the population dips to its minimum in 2014 before rising consistently. Look for the year where the slope changes from negative to positive. Choice A ignores the upward trend after 2014. Choice C overlooks the 40-unit decline followed by the 70-unit increase in that window. Choice D misinterprets sustained, accelerating growth as a temporary spike.

questions.question:
A scatter plot measures the 'Mean Reaction Time' (in milliseconds) across five age groups from 18–25 through 56–65.

Which choice best states how the visual trend on the plot relates to the aging process?
options: The graph shows a positive correlation where the slope becomes steeper in the older age demographics.
Reaction times remained stable across all groups, resulting in a horizontal line on the plot.
The youngest demographic recorded a higher reaction time than the 26–35 demographic.
The plot shows a perfectly linear relationship with a constant increase of 40 ms per group.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies the accelerating upward curve. The increases between points grow progressively larger (5, 25, 50, 90 ms), forming a J-shape rather than a straight line. Check whether the vertical gap between consecutive points stays constant or widens. Choice B is false — reaction times increase significantly. Choice C reverses the data (210 ms is lower than 215 ms). Choice D assumes constant gains contradicted by the varying increments.

questions.question:
An economist monitored unemployment rates following a factory closure in March. The graph shows monthly rates from January through June.

Which choice best describes the relationship between the timing of the closure and the resulting graph?
options: Unemployment was highest in March, the month the factory closure occurred.
The graph shows a sharp increase immediately following the closure, peaking in May before a slight decline.
The closure had no measurable impact on the unemployment trend line between March and April.
The rate in June returned to the baseline level recorded in January.
questions.explanation: Choice B accurately describes the trajectory: flat before the closure, a sharp 2.5% jump in April, a peak in May (7.2%), then a minor decrease in June. Look for the month where the line has the steepest upward angle. Choice A misidentifies the peak month. Choice C ignores the most significant visual change in the graph. Choice D is false — 6.9% remains substantially higher than January's 4.2%.

questions.question:
A physicist measured the stopping distance of a vehicle at various speeds. The graph plots stopping distance against speed from 30 km/h to 110 km/h.

Which choice best describes the findings as they would appear on the graph?
options: The graph shows that stopping distance increases at an increasing rate as speed rises.
The stopping distance remains constant once the vehicle exceeds 90 km/h.
The graph shows a negative correlation between speed and stopping distance.
The relationship is perfectly linear, with distance increasing by exactly 20 meters per interval.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies the upward-curving (quadratic) trend. The differences between distances grow (19, 27, 36, 44 meters), meaning the line connecting 90 and 110 is much steeper than the line connecting 30 and 50. Consider whether a straight line or a curve would better connect these points. Choice B is false — distance continues to climb. Choice C reverses the direction. Choice D assumes linearity contradicted by accelerating differences.

questions.question:
A climatologist tracked annual precipitation to verify a drought cycle theory. The graph shows yearly precipitation from 2016 to 2021.

Based on this graph, which statement is most accurate?
options: Precipitation levels declined consistently every year across the entire six-year period.
The graph shows a local maximum in 2017 followed by the lowest recorded precipitation in 2019.
The region maintained a stable precipitation level of approximately 5 inches each year.
The data confirms that precipitation increases by exactly 1.0 inch every four years.
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly identifies the peak (8.5 inches in 2017) and the lowest trough (2.2 inches in 2019) within the chronological sequence. Look for the highest peak and the lowest valley on the timeline. Choice A is false — levels fluctuate with clear increases in 2017, 2020, and 2021. Choice C ignores the high variability (range of 2.2 to 8.5). Choice D invents a pattern not supported by the irregular data.

questions.question:
A research team measured the 'Relative Humidity (%)' and 'Evaporation Rate (mm/day)' at a lake over several weeks. The scatter plot shows how evaporation rate changes as humidity varies from 40% to 80%.

Which choice best describes the correlation shown in the graph?
options: There is a strong negative correlation, indicating that as humidity increases, the evaporation rate decreases.
The graph shows a positive correlation, suggesting that higher humidity levels accelerate evaporation.
There is no clear correlation between humidity and evaporation in this dataset.
The evaporation rate remains constant regardless of the humidity level.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies the negative linear correlation: as humidity rises from 40% to 80%, evaporation drops from 8.2 to 1.4 mm/day. Observe whether the y-values go up or down as you move left to right along the x-axis. Choice B reverses the direction. Choice C ignores the distinct linear pattern. Choice D is false — evaporation changes by 1.7 units for every 10% humidity change.

questions.question:
A retail analyst tracked the 'Customer Footfall' at a mall during a holiday weekend. The line graph shows the number of visitors throughout the day from 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM.

Which choice best identifies the peak period of activity?
options: The graph shows a steady increase in visitors from opening until closing time.
The mall experienced its highest footfall at 2:00 PM, followed by a gradual decline in the evening.
Activity was highest at 12:00 PM before plateauing for the rest of the day.
The number of visitors at 8:00 PM was higher than the number of visitors at 10:00 AM.
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly identifies the maximum y-value (1,200 at 2:00 PM) and the subsequent downward trend. Find the coordinate with the highest value on the vertical axis. Choice A is false — the data peaks then declines significantly. Choice C ignores the 350-visitor increase from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM. Choice D is factually true (400 > 200) but does not describe the peak activity.

questions.question:
An agricultural scientist used a bar graph to compare the 'Yield (bushels per acre)' of four different fertilizer types: A, B, C, and D.

Which choice correctly interprets the comparative performance of the fertilizers?
options: Fertilizer D produced the highest yield, outperforming Fertilizer A by 40 bushels per acre.
Fertilizer B and Fertilizer D produced identical results in this study.
Fertilizer C was the least effective of the four types tested.
The average yield for all four fertilizers was exactly 100 bushels per acre.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies Fertilizer D as the maximum (160) and calculates the exact difference from Fertilizer A (120). Compare the heights of the bars for D and A to verify the 40-bushel difference. Choice B is false — 155 and 160 differ by 5. Choice C misidentifies the minimum, which is A at 120. Choice D is mathematically impossible since all values exceed 100.

questions.question:
A tech company plotted the 'Battery Life (hours)' of a smartphone against the 'Screen Brightness (%)' on a scatter plot. Brightness ranges from 20% to 100%.

Which choice best describes the relationship?
options: The graph shows a linear negative relationship where every 20% increase in brightness reduces battery life by 3 hours.
The relationship is non-linear, as the battery life drops more rapidly at higher brightness levels.
There is a positive correlation, as screen brightness and battery life increase together.
The smartphone maintains at least 10 hours of battery life at all brightness levels above 50%.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies the constant rate of change: -3 hours for every +20% brightness, creating a straight downward-sloping line. Calculate the change in battery life for each step to verify the reduction is constant. Choice B misidentifies a linear trend as non-linear. Choice C reverses the direction. Choice D is false — at 60%, 80%, and 100% brightness, battery life is 9, 6, and 3 hours, all below 10.

questions.question:
A geologist monitored 'Seismic Activity' (magnitude) near a fault line over five days. The line graph shows the daily maximum magnitude from Day 1 to Day 5.

Which choice describes the outlier or significant event in the graph?
options: Day 3 represents a significant spike in activity, as the magnitude was more than triple that of any other day.
The seismic activity increased linearly from Day 1 to Day 5.
The magnitude on Day 4 was higher than the magnitude on Day 3.
The graph shows that activity effectively ceased after the event on Day 3.
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly identifies the sharp peak on Day 3 (4.8), which stands out dramatically from surrounding values that remain below 2.2. Identify the point that looks like a sharp mountain peak compared to the rest of the line. Choice B is false — the trend rises then falls, not linear. Choice C reverses the data (4.8 > 2.1). Choice D overstates — activity continued at lower levels (2.1 and 1.8).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately interpret graph trends, identify correlations, and spot outliers across multiple chart types.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review any missed questions and focus on whether the trend is linear or non-linear, and where peaks and troughs occur.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: check the direction of the slope, calculate the rate of change, and identify the highest and lowest points.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: as the x-axis variable changes, what happens to the y-axis — and is the relationship direct, inverse, or non-linear?
